new_classes.definition_en: Spirituous beverage generally obtained by simple or successive distillations of rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with juniper berries and other aromatic substances (e.g. coriander, angelica root, anise, ginger). Only beverages with an actual alcoholic strength of at least 37.5% vol qualify for these subheadings.

new_classes.label_de: Gin in Behältern von höchstens 2 L (KN 2208 50 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Gin as defined under CN 2208 50, presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, corresponding to CN subheading 2208 50 11.

A quality borne by a spirituous beverage indicating that juniper berries (and potentially other aromatic substances) were used in its production as the defining aromatic characteristic, as required for classification as Gin under CN 2208 50.
A quality borne by a spirituous beverage indicating that its actual alcoholic strength by volume is at least 37.5%, as required for classification as Gin under CN 2208 50.

new_classes.label_en: Rectified Cereal Spirit or Ethyl Alcohol Distillation with Aromatics Process
new_classes.label_de: Destillation von rektifiziertem Getreidespirituosen oder Ethylalkohol mit Aromastoffen
new_classes.definition_en: A distillation process in which rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol are distilled (simply or successively) together with juniper berries and other aromatic substances to produce gin.

coverage_explanation: The axioms cover the main distinguishing criteria for CN 2208 50 11 (Gin in small containers ≤ 2 L): (1) Gin as a subclass of SpiritousBeverage with a characteristic juniper-berry aromatic quality, (2) the production process of distillation of rectified cereal spirits or ethyl alcohol with aromatics, (3) the minimum alcoholic strength of 37.5% vol quality, and (4) the container size constraint (≤ 2 L) via the existing SmallContainer class. A coverage gap exists because the legal note text is truncated ('For the purposes of these subheadings, only spirituous beverag…') and the exact minimum ABV threshold of 37.5% vol is inferred from standard CN/EU regulation knowledge rather than the supplied text fragment. Additionally, the disjointness restriction entry used 'complement' with owl:equivalentClass as property which is a simplification — full OWL disjointness between sibling gin process and whisky distillation process cannot be perfectly expressed in the available restriction schema. The sibling code 2208 50 19 (Gin in large containers > 2 L) is not modelled here but would use GinInLargeContainer following the same pattern.
